# Engram

> Persistent long-term memory for AI agents. Temporal knowledge graphs, cue-based recall, ACT-R inspired activation, and 17-phase offline consolidation. Open source, privacy-first, runs locally.

Engram gives AI agents one local brain that persists across sessions, projects, and compatible harnesses. The public website is operator-forward: install native Helix, connect an MCP client, bootstrap project context, inspect local storage, and run doctor before trusting the runtime.

## Recommended Startup

```bash
curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Moshik21/engram/main/scripts/install.sh | bash -s -- helix
engramctl status
engramctl storage
engramctl doctor
engramctl connect claude-code
engramctl bootstrap /path/to/project
```

Native Helix through PyO3 is the default public path: full graph, vector, and BM25 memory without Docker. Lite is the SQLite fallback/demo path. Docker full mode is an explicit legacy compatibility lane.

## OpenClaw

```bash
openclaw skills install engram-brain
curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Moshik21/engram/main/scripts/install.sh | bash -s -- openclaw
```

The OpenClaw path installs the `engram-brain` skill, configures MCP at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/mcp`, uses native Helix by default, and runs `engramctl doctor`.

## Key Capabilities

- **Operator startup**: Install -> Start -> Connect -> Bootstrap -> Inspect Storage -> Verify Doctor
- **Lifecycle**: Capture -> Cue -> Project -> Recall -> Consolidate
- **Native Helix**: PyO3 in-process graph/vector/BM25 backend with no Docker requirement
- **Fallbacks**: Lite is SQLite fallback/demo; Docker full is explicit legacy compatibility
- **CQRS ingestion**: `observe` for fast cueable capture, `remember` for full extraction
- **Activation-aware recall**: recency, frequency, relevance, graph structure, and cue signals
- **17-phase consolidation**: triage, merge, calibrate, infer, evidence_adjudication, edge_adjudication, replay, prune, compact, mature, semanticize, schema, reindex, graph_embed, microglia, immunity, dream
- **Storage visibility**: `engramctl storage` and `/api/storage` show resolved paths, usage, counts, and growth
- **MCP server**: 27 tools for capture, recall, memory authority, evaluation, management, and system control
- **Privacy-first**: memory storage is local; no centralized data collection

## Trust Surfaces

- Native Helix default storage: `~/.helix/engram-native`
- Lite default storage: `~/.engram/engram.db`
- Basic deterministic capture/recall can run without an API key; richer extraction can use Anthropic or local Ollama.
- `engramctl uninstall` removes the install while preserving data by default.
- `engramctl uninstall --purge-data` is the explicit delete-data path.
- Dashboard and CLI surfaces expose storage diagnostics, lifecycle state, and doctor readiness.

## MCP Tools

Capture: observe, remember, observe_image, observe_file, adjudicate_evidence, forget, bootstrap_project
Retrieval: recall, search_entities, search_facts, search_artifacts, get_context, route_question
Authority/evaluation: claim_authority, get_runtime_state, get_lifecycle_summary, get_evaluation_report, record_recall_evaluation, record_session_continuity_evaluation
Management: mark_identity_core, feedback, intend, dismiss_intention, list_intentions
System: get_graph_state, get_consolidation_status, trigger_consolidation
